summary:
A bug in the leader-election logic allowed a node to declare itself leader with only half
(≤ N⁄2) of the votes, so two different partitions of the same cluster could both obtain a
“quorum” and elect independent leaders (“split-brain”).

Fix – require a strict majority:  

app/cluster/node.go, in StartElection:

```go
// Before (wrong – allows N/2):
// if finalVotes >= totalNodes/2 {

// After (correct – > N/2):
majority := totalNodes/2 + 1
if finalVotes >= majority {
    n.State = Leader
}
```

With the change every election needs > N⁄2 votes (3 in a 5-node cluster), so the
smaller partition cannot form a quorum and split-brain is no longer possible.

Run the supplied script:

```
$ bash test.sh
=== Split-Brain Leader Election Test ===
--- Round 1 ---
Round 1: OK
--- Round 2 ---
Round 2: OK
--- Round 3 ---
Round 3: OK
PASS: No split-brain detected across all rounds
```

The implementation now passes the safety test.
